selected_route_mode: tile-only native injection (model on HIP, attention tile via AMDISARenderer)
hidden_fallback_check: no HIP/LLVM block tile, no owned tile in candidate route
hip_block_tile_baseline_note: comparator is the owned hand-AMDGCN tile; HIP-generated block-tile baseline is in bench/qk-owned-oracle-parity (separate harness)
baseline_analysis: native tile ~0.4% of owned -- EXPECTED ground-truth baseline (Phase I allowed to be slow). Dominant bottleneck = grid=[1,1,1]: the tile kvh/s are RANGE(GLOBAL) which the native backend lowers to SERIAL loops (global_size derives from SPECIAL only), so ONE workgroup does all Hkv*S splits serially, vs the owned tile Hkv*S parallel workgroups. Grid parallelism + waitcnt(J) + scheduling(K) + modulo(L) are the levers; out of scope for Phase I.
